source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Miovision:

At Miovision, we’re unlocking transportation networks that move you. Our vision and mission is to enable smart, fast, safe communities that simply flow, as we drive the Intelligent Mobility Revolution. Backed by the world’s most advanced traffic AI, Miovision’s innovations in traffic signal planning and operations are making it possible for cities to improve the transportation experience for drivers, cyclists and pedestrians.

Our values drive us. They’re at the core of everything we do. If they align with yours, proceed through the GREEN light!

 - All in to win: We're driven by a winning mindset, approaching every challenge with intensity, clarity, and speed.

 - One Miovision: We succeed as one team, uniting diverse talents, building on trust, and putting our shared mission before ego.

 - Be better every day: We're committed to continuous growth, staying curious, building mastery, and embracing challenges as learning opportunities.

 - Make it happen: We are proactive and results-driven, taking ownership, acting with urgency, and focusing on solutions that deliver real impact.

 - Earn the customer: We are deeply customer-centric, focused on earning our customers' partnership every day by delivering exceptional experiences that drive their success.

Position Summary:

At Miovision, we are engineering the future of smart transportation, but to keep our app business moving at top speed, we need a highly strategic Senior Product Manager to take the wheel. Reporting directly to the Product Director of App Services, you will navigate the complex intersections of our detection, performance, and traffic optimization portfolio. We are looking for someone who can see through the fog of domain ambiguity, mapping out a clear route that transforms vague market needs and intangible user behaviors into concrete solutions. You will be the driver shaping our industry—shifting our products out of the slow lane of basic data visualization and accelerating into automated optimization. If you are ready to steer our software and hardware ecosystem to solve real-world infrastructure challenges without hitting a gridlock, green means go!



Objectives and Responsibilities:

 - Own the Strategy: Take full accountability for the success and adoption metrics of your product area, looking beyond immediate feature sets to define how our software and hardware seamlessly integrate.

 - See the Future: Predict 1-2 year industry shifts, initiate de-risking projects today, and distill massive market complexity into a crystal-clear "North Star" for your team.

 - Master the Economics: Proactively design solutions that address the "cost of scale." Pivot strategies to drastically reduce deployment friction and improve unit economics across your entire product line.

 - Turn Ambiguity into Action: Take ill-defined problems within the detection and optimization space, evaluate highly variable factors, and guide them toward compelling, actionable solutions with high-conviction business cases backed by a robust 12-month roadmap.

 - Establish Discovery Rhythms: Build a permanent rhythm of discovery to inform your long-term plans. Ensure your team builds with absolute conviction by validating the "riskiest assumptions" first.

 - Leverage AI & Data: Define exactly how AI and complex data insights can transform the user experience, transitioning our offerings from reactive data visualization to automated, predictive optimization.

 - Make High-Stakes Decisions: Spearhead major innovations, architectures, and key business decisions where the consequence of error is severe. Direct product investments based on a deep understanding of market-wide economic trends and P&L goals.

 - Shift the Culture: Act as a trusted advisor to senior leadership and play a pivotal role in shifting the organization toward a culture that is inherently Product-Led and Sales-Supported.

 - Align and Mentor: Build formal networks with key industry executives. Successfully

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive, Opticom Solutions
source_jobs.description:
About Miovision:

At Miovision, we’re unlocking transportation networks that move you. Our vision and mission is to enable smart, fast, safe communities that simply flow, as we drive the Intelligent Mobility Revolution. Backed by the world’s most advanced traffic AI, Miovision’s innovations in traffic signal planning and operations are making it possible for cities to improve the transportation experience for drivers, cyclists and pedestrians.

Our values drive us. They’re at the core of everything we do. If they align with yours, proceed through the GREEN light!

 - All in to win: We're driven by a winning mindset, approaching every challenge with intensity, clarity, and speed.

 - One Miovision: We succeed as one team, uniting diverse talents, building on trust, and putting our shared mission before ego.

 - Be better every day: We're committed to continuous growth, staying curious, building mastery, and embracing challenges as learning opportunities.

 - Make it happen: We are proactive and results-driven, taking ownership, acting with urgency, and focusing on solutions that deliver real impact.

 - Earn the customer: We are deeply customer-centric, focused on earning our customers' partnership every day by delivering exceptional experiences that drive their success.

Position Summary:

Miovision’s Opticom https://miovision.com/opticom/ product has been providing best-in-class priority control solutions for over 58 years. A testament to Opticom’s quality and durability is the continuing growth in the customer base, now at over 180,000 intersections and vehicles with over 5,000 customers worldwide. Our success is based on working with communities to provide the best emergency vehicle preemption and transit signal priority systems.

As an Enterprise Account Executive for Opticom Solutions, you'll be responsible for identifying, developing, and closing high-value business opportunities across Florida's 1st Responder and Transit markets, while also maintaining momentum of our existing relationships to expand their accounts. You will move beyond a traditional transactional sales approach to operate as a critical, strategic business partner. Your mission is to ensure Miovision always gets the green light and help cities improve the safety, reliability, and efficiency of their emergency and transit vehicles, optimize traffic flow, reduce congestion, and enhance smart mobility.

Objectives and Responsibilities:

 - Deliver Results: Achieve or exceed quarterly and annual revenue targets by prioritizing high-value prospects and key accounts within a defined territory.

 - Own the Sales Lifecycle: Lead complex, multi-layered enterprise sales cycles, from discovery and needs analysis to contract negotiation and closing.

 - Build Pipeline: Make prospecting part of your regular routine.

 - Account Lifecycle Ambassador: Act as an ambassador to enterprise accounts, continuously tracking initial sale success, managing emerging client needs, and maintaining strong, long-term customer satisfaction.

 - Strategic Forecasting and Growth: Develop and execute territory plans including market assumptions, forecasting, risks, and mitigation strategies.

 - Product Champion: Act as the region’s subject matter expert for Miovision Opticom products and services. Champion the transition from hardware-based to cloud-based EVP systems.

 - Trusted Advisor: Become a trusted advisor to city departments to uncover their needs and articulate the value of Miovision's solutions.

 - Strategic Opportunity Management: Lead the coordination and development of RFP and RFI responses to position Miovision as the preferred vendor for modern traffic solutions.

 - Maintain Data Integrity: Use Salesforce with discipline to provide leadership with accurate forecasts and clear visibility into your pipeline.

 - Industry Trends: Stay sharp on market challenges and the competitive landscape to effectively position Miovision’s value proposition and feed critical intellige

source_jobs.title: Account Executive, Traffic Engineering (Nordics, UK, Netherlands)
source_jobs.description:
About Miovision:

At Miovision, we’re unlocking transportation networks that move you. Our vision and mission is to enable smart, fast, safe communities that simply flow, as we drive the Intelligent Mobility Revolution. Backed by the world’s most advanced traffic AI, Miovision’s innovations in traffic signal planning and operations are making it possible for cities to improve the transportation experience for drivers, cyclists and pedestrians.

Our values drive us. They’re at the core of everything we do. If they align with yours, proceed through the GREEN light!

 - All in to win: We're driven by a winning mindset, approaching every challenge with intensity, clarity, and speed.

 - One Miovision: We succeed as one team, uniting diverse talents, building on trust, and putting our shared mission before ego.

 - Be better every day: We're committed to continuous growth, staying curious, building mastery, and embracing challenges as learning opportunities.

 - Make it happen: We are proactive and results-driven, taking ownership, acting with urgency, and focusing on solutions that deliver real impact.

 - Earn the customer: We are deeply customer-centric, focused on earning our customers' partnership every day by delivering exceptional experiences that drive their success.

Position Summary:

Cities are living, breathing organisms, and right now, many of them are stuck in gridlock. As an Account Executive at Miovision, you are the primary driver of the "green light." Your mission is to help municipalities trade traffic frustrations for smart, fluid urban environments that simply flow. You aren't just selling tech; you are clearing the path for safer intersections and steering initiatives that directly reduce congestion.

In this role, you will be responsible for identifying, qualifying, and closing new business opportunities within a defined territory. You will act as the primary point of contact for municipal stakeholders, driving the adoption of Miovision's Traffic Engineering products (hardware and software) by aligning our solutions with their specific infrastructure goals.

Territory Coverage: Nordics (Finland, Norway, Sweden, Denmark), UK, and Netherlands

Objectives and Responsibilities:

 - Deliver Results: Achieve or exceed quarterly and annual revenue targets by prioritizing high-value prospects within a defined territory.

 - Own the Sales Lifecycle: Independently manage the end-to-end sales process, from discovery and needs analysis to contract negotiation and closing.

 - Build your Pipeline: Make prospecting part of your regular routine.

 - Strategic Forecasting and Growth: Develop and execute territory plans that maintain a robust, multi-stage pipeline of opportunities.

 - Product Champion: Champion our full suite of solutions, including Traffic Planning, Traffic Operations, Emergency Vehicle Preemption, Transit Signal Priority, and any other solutions available to market in the territory.

 - Consult with Purpose: Act as a trusted advisor to city departments to uncover their needs and articulate the value of Miovision's solutions.

 - Strategic Opportunity Management: Lead the coordination and development of RFP and RFI responses to position Miovision as the preferred vendor for modern traffic solutions.

 - Maintain Data Integrity: Use Salesforce with discipline to provide leadership with accurate forecasts and clear visibility into your pipeline.

 - Industry Trends: Stay sharp on market challenges and the competitive landscape to effectively position Miovision’s value proposition.

 - Ecosystem Collaboration: Work collaboratively with Partners, Business Development and Marketing teams to launch regional campaigns. Engage an array of external influencers such as City Departments, City Mobility, Open Data, Engineering Firms, Academics, Consultants, etc.

 - Brand Ambassador: Represent Miovision in person on site with clients and at major trade shows. Travel up to 30%.

Ideal Profile:

 - Specialized Sales Knowl

source_jobs.title: Technical Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Miovision:

At Miovision, we’re unlocking transportation networks that move you. Our vision and mission is to enable smart, fast, safe communities that simply flow, as we drive the Intelligent Mobility Revolution. Backed by the world’s most advanced traffic AI, Miovision’s innovations in traffic signal planning and operations are making it possible for cities to improve the transportation experience for drivers, cyclists and pedestrians.

Our values drive us. They’re at the core of everything we do. If they align with yours, proceed through the GREEN light!

 - All in to win: We're driven by a winning mindset, approaching every challenge with intensity, clarity, and speed.

 - One Miovision: We succeed as one team, uniting diverse talents, building on trust, and putting our shared mission before ego.

 - Be better every day: We're committed to continuous growth, staying curious, building mastery, and embracing challenges as learning opportunities.

 - Make it happen: We are proactive and results-driven, taking ownership, acting with urgency, and focusing on solutions that deliver real impact.

 - Earn the customer: We are deeply customer-centric, focused on earning our customers' partnership every day by delivering exceptional experiences that drive their success.

Position Summary:

As a Technical Customer Success Manager (CSM) at Miovision, you will own the post-sales customer relationships, tracks customer health, and drives revenue growth. This role engages directly with customers to help them unlock the full value of our traffic technology. You will share industry best practices, help solve problems, and provide technical guidance. Ultimately, your goal is to ensure that onboarding, adopting, and using Miovision feels as seamless as hitting every green light.

Territory & Location: This role manages our Southeast US territory (covering OK, AR, LA, MS, TN, AL, GA, FL, NC, SC). To keep close to our customers, candidates must be located within one of these states.

Objectives and Responsibilities:

 - Own the Customer Journey: Manage relationships from onboarding through renewal to maximize retention and drive long-term revenue growth.

 - Build Trusted Relationships: Establish deep, lasting connections with executive sponsors, key municipal contacts, and daily end-users.

 - Navigate Risk Proactively: Continually monitor account health metrics, risks, escalations, and opportunities to steer clients toward optimal outcomes.

 - Advocate Internally: Advocate for customers internally and work with cross-functional counterparts to improve the customer experience. Coordinate with the product team on documentation, customer enablement, and product specifications.

 - Accelerate Value Realization: Create and deliver clear, accessible training content so customers realize immediate ROI from their investment.

 - Customer Support: Support customer solution design, technical documentation, and integration implementation plans to ensure adoption of the Miovision solutions.

 - Bridge Adoption Gaps: Identify precisely where a customer is stuck versus where they want to be, creating clear action plans to bridge the gap and expand their network.

 - Technical Problem Solving: Resolve complex product issues for customers and the distributor network.

 - Travel: Travel is required up to 30% across the US and Canada to provide on-site consulting work to clients.

Ideal Profile:

 - Proven Track Record: You have an established track record of success in account management, project management, installation, or configuration in a technical field supporting customers in a business-to-business (B2B) or business-to-government (B2G) environment.

 - Skilled Communicator: You have exceptional oral and written communication skills with the natural ability to communicate across all levels of an organization.

 - Strong Consulting Skills: Skilled at consulting including team facilitation, business case development, business analysis, process map

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Miovision:

At Miovision, we’re unlocking transportation networks that move you. Our vision and mission is to enable smart, fast, safe communities that simply flow, as we drive the Intelligent Mobility Revolution. Backed by the world’s most advanced traffic AI, Miovision’s innovations in traffic signal planning and operations are making it possible for cities to improve the transportation experience for drivers, cyclists and pedestrians.

Our values drive us. They’re at the core of everything we do. If they align with yours, proceed through the GREEN light!

 - All in to win: We're driven by a winning mindset, approaching every challenge with intensity, clarity, and speed.

 - One Miovision: We succeed as one team, uniting diverse talents, building on trust, and putting our shared mission before ego.

 - Be better every day: We're committed to continuous growth, staying curious, building mastery, and embracing challenges as learning opportunities.

 - Make it happen: We are proactive and results-driven, taking ownership, acting with urgency, and focusing on solutions that deliver real impact.

 - Earn the customer: We are deeply customer-centric, focused on earning our customers' partnership every day by delivering exceptional experiences that drive their success.

Position Summary:

At Miovision, we are paving the way for the future of smart transportation, and we need a Product Marketing Manager who can help our products merge seamlessly into the fast lane of the market. Reporting to the Director of Product Marketing, you will act as the ultimate traffic controller for our Go-To-Market (GTM) strategies. You will steer cross-functional teams, clear roadblocks to revenue growth, and ensure our new product launches never hit a red light. From mapping out customer needs and navigating market trends to fine-tuning pricing, messaging, and positioning, you are the GPS guiding our products to success. You will carpool with Product Management, CRO leadership, Sales, and Marketing to build high-octane campaigns and arm our sales fleet with the tools they need to win. If you have the strategic horsepower, creative fuel, and analytical precision to keep us miles ahead of the competition, it is time to shift into high gear!



Objectives and Responsibilities:

 - Messaging and Positioning: Craft compelling, highly differentiated product narratives that deeply resonate with target audiences and effectively broadcast our value proposition.

 - Go-To-Market Strategy: Collaborate seamlessly with cross-functional teams to design and implement high-impact GTM strategies, ensuring coordinated and spectacular product launches.

 - Cross-Functional Alignment: Act as the strategic connective tissue between product management, sales, marketing, customer support, and operations to lock in alignment on strategy and execution.

 - Product Launch Mastery: Plan, orchestrate, and meticulously measure the rollout of new products across the organization.

 - Performance Analysis: Monitor critical performance metrics—such as market share, sales velocity, and customer satisfaction—to evaluate campaign effectiveness and iterate for success.

 - Market Research & Sizing: Dive deep into market data to identify customer needs, industry trends, and competitive landscapes, using these insights to inform positioning and thought leadership.

 - Customer Discovery: Partner closely with Product Management to translate raw customer feedback into actionable product and GTM enhancements.

 - Competitive Intelligence: Stay vigilant on industry trends and competitor movements, providing stakeholders with the sharp insights and content needed to improve roadmaps and boost win-rates.

The Ideal Profile:

 - You have a strong, proven background in content creation, from writing magnetic copy and building compelling presentations to developing a wide array of marketing collateral.

 - You bring a highly creative mindset, always looking to conceptualize and execute unique

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive – Intelligent Transportation Systems (California)
source_jobs.description:
About Miovision:

At Miovision, we’re unlocking transportation networks that move you. Our vision and mission is to enable smart, fast, safe communities that simply flow, as we drive the Intelligent Mobility Revolution. Backed by the world’s most advanced traffic AI, Miovision’s innovations in traffic signal planning and operations are making it possible for cities to improve the transportation experience for drivers, cyclists and pedestrians.

Our values drive us. They’re at the core of everything we do. If they align with yours, proceed through the GREEN light!

 - All in to win: We're driven by a winning mindset, approaching every challenge with intensity, clarity, and speed.

 - One Miovision: We succeed as one team, uniting diverse talents, building on trust, and putting our shared mission before ego.

 - Be better every day: We're committed to continuous growth, staying curious, building mastery, and embracing challenges as learning opportunities.

 - Make it happen: We are proactive and results-driven, taking ownership, acting with urgency, and focusing on solutions that deliver real impact.

 - Earn the customer: We are deeply customer-centric, focused on earning our customers' partnership every day by delivering exceptional experiences that drive their success.

Position Summary:
As a Senior Account Executive in California for Miovision's Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Division, you’ll act as the strategic lead for your territory. You will drive regional revenue by cultivating high-level government relationships and delivering complex, hybrid solutions to modernize city infrastructure. You’ll serve as a lead driver for high-value accounts, navigating multi-year procurement cycles and shaping market specifications to help our clients build cities that simply flow.

Please Note: This is a remote opportunity for candidates located in California.

Objectives and Responsibilities:

 - Own the Region: You’ll develop the strategic roadmap to hit revenue goals and expand Miovision’s presence across California by developing and executing a comprehensive regional strategic plan; penetrating complex accounts requiring long-term, multi-phased planning. You'll manage full cycle sales - from prospecting to closing.

 - Strategic Opportunity Management: You’ll lead the charge on joint RFP responses and navigate complex opportunities to shape specifications toward Miovision’s solutions.

 - Executive Influence: You will cultivate a broad network of influence across the traffic ecosystem, engaging with executive-level buying committees, City Departments, Engineering Firms, Academic experts, and more to build consensus and guide accounts through the complex public-sector journey to drive long-term adoption.

 - Collaborate to Win: Lead cross-functional “deal teams” (including Public Safety, Customer Success, and Solution Engineering), serving as a definitive internal thought leader to help you close new business and expand existing accounts.

 - Advise and Educate: Become the go-to subject matter expert on ITS solutions, helping city leaders understand the future of "Intelligent Mobility," articulating technical value propositions and influencing product roadmaps based on market feedback.

 - Market Insight and Positioning: Monitor shifting industry trends, competitor movements, and technical evolutions to effectively differentiate the Miovision value proposition within the ITS landscape.

 - Strategic Forecasting and Growth: Develop comprehensive sales action plans and rigorous monthly, quarterly, and annual forecasting models to ensure predictable pipeline growth and regional scale.

 - Maintain Data Integrity: Use Salesforce with discipline to provide leadership with accurate forecasts and clear visibility into your pipeline.

 - Compliance: Navigate the regulatory framework and Approved Products List process to ensure ability to sell into all segments of the market.

 - Travel: You'll travel up to 40% of the time to meet

notes: No tech roles posted, mostly sales and marketing roles
